metadata.name: Strike It Rich creates Treasure token; token tapped and sacrificed for mana
metadata.description: p1 casts Strike It Rich ({R} Sorcery), it resolves and creates a Treasure token on the battlefield per CR 111.10a. p1 then activates the Treasure's mana ability ({T}, Sacrifice this token: Add one mana of any color) by issuing a tap_for_mana command. The mana ability resolves immediately without using the stack (CR 605.3b). The Treasure token is tapped and sacrificed as a cost (CR 602.2c), moving to the graveyard, then ceasing to exist as a state-based action (CR 111.7). One colorless mana is added to p1's pool (any-color defaults to colorless per engine implementation).
metadata.generation_notes: Strike It Rich is a Sorcery (not Instant) costing {R} (verified via MCP lookup_card). Its only effect is CreateToken with treasure_token_spec(1) per definitions.rs:2307-2314. The Treasure token has a mana ability (sacrifice_self: true, any_color: true, requires_tap: true) per ManaAbility::treasure() — CR 111.10a: '{T}, Sacrifice this token: Add one mana of any color.' Because this is a mana ability (CR 605.1a: no target, adds mana, not loyalty), it does NOT go on the stack and cannot be countered (CR 605.3b). The sacrifice is a cost paid at activation time (CR 602.2c), so the token is in the graveyard before any mana is added. The token then ceases to exist as a state-based action (CR 111.7 / 704.5d: tokens in non-battlefield zones cease to exist). The engine currently defaults 'any color' to colorless (consistent with AddManaAnyColor in effects/mod.rs and the ability-plan), so mana_pool ends with colorless: 1. The tap_for_mana action targets the Treasure token on the battlefield (ability_index 0 = the single mana ability). Strike It Rich then goes to p1's graveyard (cast normally, not via flashback, so it goes to graveyard not exile). Sorcery timing: p1 is active player in precombat main phase with an empty stack — valid sorcery window per CR 307.1.

script.step_note: p1 casts Strike It Rich from hand

actions.description: Initial state: Strike It Rich in p1 hand, battlefield empty (except Mountain), stack empty
actions.note: p1 has {R} in pool from initial_state; Mountain is tapped implicitly by mana_pool setup
CR 601.2: p1 casts Strike It Rich (Sorcery {R}); valid sorcery timing — active player, main phase, empty stack (CR 307.1). {R} paid from mana pool.

actions.description: After casting: Strike It Rich on stack, hand empty
actions.note: Strike It Rich moved from hand to stack as a spell object
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — top of stack resolves.

resolution.note: CR 111.10a: Creates one colorless Treasure artifact token with '{T}, Sacrifice this token: Add one mana of any color.' Token enters battlefield under p1's control (CR 111.1, 111.5).

actions.note: CR 608.2: Sorcery resolves; CreateToken effect executes; Strike It Rich moves to p1's graveyard (cast normally, not via flashback, so not exiled).

actions.description: After resolution: Treasure token on p1's battlefield, Strike It Rich in p1 graveyard, stack empty
actions.note: Battlefield now has Mountain + Treasure token. Strike It Rich in graveyard (not exiled — normal cast, not flashback cast).

script.step_note: p1 taps and sacrifices Treasure token for mana

actions.note: CR 605.3b: Treasure's '{T}, Sacrifice this token: Add one mana of any color' is a mana ability (CR 605.1a: no target, adds mana, not loyalty). It does NOT go on the stack and resolves immediately. Sacrifice is a cost paid at activation (CR 602.2c): Treasure moves to graveyard before mana is added. Engine defaults 'any color' to colorless.

CR 704.5d / CR 111.7: A token in a zone other than the battlefield ceases to exist as a state-based action. The Treasure token was moved to the graveyard as the sacrifice cost, so it now ceases to exist.
SBAs checked after mana ability activation (CR 704.3: checked whenever a player would receive priority).

actions.description: Final state: Treasure token sacrificed and gone; p1 has 1 colorless mana in pool
actions.note: Battlefield has only Mountain remaining (Treasure token was sacrificed as cost and then ceased to exist per SBA). p1 mana pool has 1 colorless mana from Treasure activation. Any-color mana defaults to colorless in the engine per ability-plan-treasure-tokens.md Decision 3 / mana.rs implementation.
